title: GetResponse vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: GetResponse vs Constant Contact: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
h1: GetResponse vs Constant Contact: Side-by-Side Comparison (2025)
intro: Choosing between GetResponse and Constant Contact comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. GetResponse is built for online course creators and webinar hosts, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and local businesses. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Online Course Creators And Webinar Hosts

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on online course creators and webinar hosts
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on nonprofits and local businesses

faq.q: Is GetResponse better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. GetResponse wins for online course creators and webinar hosts, while Constant Contact is stronger for nonprofits and local businesses. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact starts at a lower price point ($12/mo vs $19/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Constant Contact to GetResponse is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
